lines.en_lit: When was asked, the question was asked about Og, king of Bashan, it is Beit Shammai stringent,

Rabbi Zeira strongly objects to this What is different eating, that all people volume of a large date; and what is different drinking, where each and every according to his own measure, Abaye said to him: The Sages have tradition the volume of the large date, that this settles his mind, less than this does not settle drinking, his mind is settled with his own but his mind is not settled of his fellow

Rabbi Zeira strongly objects to this Is everyone with the volume of a large date, and Og, king of Bashan, with the volume of a large date? Abaye said to him: The Sages have tradition that this settles his mind, less than this does not settle his mind. However, everyone greatly Og, king of Bashan, a little

Rabbi Zeira strongly objects to this fatty meat, volume of a large date, grapevine shoots, with the volume of a large date? Abaye said to him: The Sages have tradition that with this one’s mind is settled, with less than this his mind is not settled. However, fatty meat, greatly grapevine shoots, a little

Rava strongly objects to this: of an olive-bulk within to eat a half-loaf of bread. volume of a large date, within to eat a half-loaf of bread. Abaye said to him: The Sages have tradition that this his mind is settled; but a longer his mind is not settled,

Rava strongly objects to this: volume of a large date within to eat a half-loaf of bread; half of a half-loaf, within to eat a half-loaf of bread. Rav Pappa said to him: Leave ritual impurity of the body because that is not by Torah law

But did Rav Pappa say that But Isn’t it written: neither shall you make yourselves impure with them, that you should be impure thereby” And Rav Pappa said: From here, ritual impurity of the body is by Torah law. rabbinic and the verse is a mere support.

All foods Rav Pappa said: ate meat and salt combine Although is not eating, since people do eat they combine Reish Lakish said: Brine on a vegetable combines volume of a large date on Yom Kippur. It obvious. Lest you say a beverage, it teaches us any item that prepares food is a food.
Reish Lakish said: One who eats an excessive manner on Yom Kippur, is exempt, What is the reason was written shall not be afflicted excluding one who harms

Rabbi Yirmeya said Reish Lakish said: A non-priest who ate teruma in an excessive manner pays the principal, and does not pay the fifth, if eat excludes harming Rabbi Yirmeya said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: A non-priest
